seoTitle: Daveyston vs Daw Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Daveyston vs Daw Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Daveyston and Daw Park.
heading: Daveyston vs Daw Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Daveyston, South Australia and Daw Park, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Daveyston has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Daw Park (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Daveyston or Daw Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Daveyston has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Daw Park scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Daveyston scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Daw Park scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Daveyston or Daw Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Daveyston has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
